H1_Hospicing.text: Can regulate personal nervous system responses during high-stress climate anxiety or ecological grief episodes.
Can synthesize personal shadow aspects triggered by systemic collapse into actionable empathy for others.
Can consciously uncouple personal self-worth from capitalist metrics of productivity and infinite growth.
Can identify and safely observe one's own defensive mechanisms when confronted with systemic ecological privilege.
Can maintain somatic presence (grounding) while processing complex, overwhelming climate data.
Can rapidly identify and dissolve personal eco-saviorism complexes before they impact community dynamics.
Can hold compassionate boundaries when interacting with individuals suffering from acute climate denial.
Can process the moral injury of participating in extractive industries while transitioning to regenerative livelihoods.
Can practice radical forgiveness toward those entrenched in H1 (legacy) destructive systems.
Can identify the subtle ways imperialist and colonial logic permeates one's own thought patterns.
Can self-regulate through breathwork or meditation before entering high-stakes policy negotiations.
Can identify one's core triggers related to scarcity and pre-emptively manage them in communal settings.
Can observe and document one's own shifting baseline syndrome regarding local ecological degradation.
Can cultivate a mindset of 'enoughness' to actively resist hyper-consumerist conditioning.
Can identify and untangle one's own complicity in systemic oppression without falling into paralysis.
Can practice 'witnessing'—observing ecological destruction without immediately jumping to solutionism, to fully grasp scale.
Can map one's personal consumption habits directly to the specific global sacrifice zones they depend on.
Can maintain a state of 'relaxed alertness' during long, high-tension direct action blockades.
Can maintain a daily practice of gratitude specifically directed at invisible infrastructural workers (sanitation, grid).
Can practice 'Digital Minimalism', deliberately curating information diets to preserve cognitive bandwidth.

H2_Bridging.text: Can cultivate and sustain 'beginner's mind' when analyzing failing legacy systems to avoid cognitive bias.
Can maintain a state of 'active hope' without falling into toxic positivity or paralyzing doomism.
Can track personal energy inputs/outputs to prevent burnout during intense phase-shifts in project development.
Can transition from a 'scarcity mindset' to an 'abundance mindset' when evaluating local bio-capacity.
Can conduct a personal 'energy audit' to align daily tasks with one's natural circadian and seasonal rhythms.
Can sit in the discomfort of 'not knowing' without prematurely rushing to false, mechanistic solutions.
Can transmute anger at environmental injustice into sustained, focused, strategic action.
Can intentionally cycle between focused analytical thinking and diffuse, divergent systems thinking.
Can map out one's personal 'ikigai' directly onto the needs of the local bioregion.
Can consciously embrace failure as a necessary evolutionary feedback loop rather than a personal deficit.
Can integrate grief as a permanent, healthy companion rather than an emotion to be 'cured'.
Can experience joy and celebration without guilt in the midst of global polycrisis.
Can practice 'inward mobility'—finding depth and exploration without geographical travel.
Can rapidly unlearn industrial-era efficiency paradigms when evaluating the success of a food forest.
Can hold the 'tragic gap' between the reality of climate collapse and the vision of a regenerative future without burning out.
Can identify when one is operating from a 'trauma response' versus a 'strategic response' in organizing.
Can let go of the need for absolute certainty before taking action in complex, chaotic environments.
Can cultivate a sense of profound playfulness and humor even when discussing existential threats.
Can maintain radical empathy for politicians or executives who are actively perpetuating the crisis.
Can map one's own lineage and ancestral traumas to understand inherited behavioral patterns toward nature.
Can consciously hold the tension of being an 'outsider' when trying to support local Indigenous-led movements.
Can deliberately practice 'doing nothing' as a radical intervention against capitalist productivity paradigms.
Can identify and transmute the 'savior complex' into a posture of deep solidarity and mutual aid.
Can recognize and bypass the 'Dunning-Kruger effect' when learning entirely new ecological disciplines.
Can hold the cognitive dissonance of simultaneously dismantling old systems while building radically new ones.
Can map how historical, generational trauma influences current organizational leadership behaviors.
Can recognize projection and transference when navigating power dynamics in grassroots organizing.
Can intentionally step out of the 'rescuer' role in the Karpman Drama Triangle during community disputes.
Can detach personal identity from a specific project's success to prevent founder's syndrome.
Can shift from a 'hero/savior' leadership archetype to a 'host/weaver' archetype in community settings.

H3_Radical.text: Can sustain a daily practice of deep ecological listening and biomimetic contemplation to guide decision-making.
Can consciously map and continuously update one's own worldview and epistemological blind spots.
Can practice deep time awareness, holding the perspective of ancestors 7 generations past and descendants 7 generations future.
Can routinely integrate intuition and dream-work into formal strategic planning processes.
Can intentionally shift one's baseline state of consciousness to perceive landscapes as animate, living entities.
Can adopt a 'mycelial' self-concept, viewing oneself strictly as a node in a larger relational network.
Can deliberately cultivate awe and reverence for local micro-ecosystems (e.g., moss, lichen, soil life).
Can sustain personal motivation entirely decoupled from external validation or financial reward.
Can hold the paradox of individual insignificance and absolute individual necessity in global transition.
Can maintain profound patience during the multi-year lag phases of ecological restoration.
Can systematically dismantle internalized anthropocentrism in daily decision-making.
Can practice sensory expansion to notice subtle shifts in wind, humidity, and animal behavior.
Can align personal spiritual or philosophical beliefs directly with material ecological stewardship.
Can track how diet and local food consumption alter one's own psychological state and groundedness.
Can cultivate a sense of deep security grounded in community networks rather than financial accumulation.
Can integrate psychedelic or non-ordinary states of consciousness into formal ecological design processes.
Can practice 'interbeing'—experiencing the self as deeply entangled with all other life forms.
Can deliberately fast or alter diet for spiritual connection to the land and awareness of privilege.
Can consciously observe the flow of water during a storm to intuitively understand local hydrology.
Can consciously uncouple personal security from the state and couple it to local mutual aid networks.
Can intuitively read the weather patterns from cloud formations and wind shifts.
Can consciously hold space for 'Eutopia' (the good place) rather than 'Utopia' (the no place).
Can map and track the shifting phenomenological experience of time during deep immersion in nature.
Can cultivate a sense of kinship with non-human entities (e.g., rivers, mountains) as equal subjects.

H1_Hospicing.text: Can perform a baseline energy audit of a home using thermal imaging and blower door tests.
Can repair a puncture, true a wheel, and replace the drivetrain on a standard bicycle.
Can conduct a basic soil texture by feel test and a mason jar soil composition test.
Can tie 5 essential load-bearing and tensioning knots for construction and rigging.
Can safely fell a medium-sized tree using proper notching and hinging techniques.
Can splice electrical wires and read basic circuit diagrams to repair household appliances.
Can set up a drip irrigation system with timers and pressure regulators for water conservation.
Can perform a soil percolation test to determine the viability of a site for a septic system or rain garden.
Can build and maintain a worm farm (vermicompost) to process indoor household food waste.
Can install an induction cooktop and disconnect a residential gas line safely to electrify a kitchen.
Can test local water sources for pH, dissolved oxygen, and basic contaminants using field kits.
Can design a simple database in SQL to track a local food co-op's inventory.
Can calculate the embodied carbon, operational energy, and lifecycle ecological footprint of a proposed community building.
Can map and categorize the urban tree canopy using open-source satellite imagery and QGIS.

H2_Bridging.text: Can install, calibrate, and maintain a residential-scale greywater filtration and irrigation system.
Can prune and graft foundational fruit tree species to optimize yield and disease resistance in a food forest.
Can build and maintain a thermal mass rocket mass heater using locally sourced earthen materials.
Can construct a simple, low-cost biochar retort and properly inoculate the resulting char.
Can install and configure a basic off-grid solar PV system (panels, charge controller, battery, inverter).
Can build a hugelkultur raised bed using locally sourced decaying wood and organic matter.
Can construct a rainwater catchment system with first-flush diverters and proper overflow management.
Can weld basic structural joints (MIG/TIG) for repairing agricultural or upcycled equipment.
Can build a passive solar greenhouse optimized for the specific winter sun angles of the latitude.
Can build a simple compost heated shower system for off-grid hygiene.
Can construct an A-frame level to accurately map contour lines on a hillside.
Can properly insulate a loft space using natural materials like sheep's wool or hempcrete.
Can construct a cob wall mixing clay, sand, and straw to the correct structural consistency.
Can draft an accurate scaled site plan using CAD or hand-drafting tools.
Can propagate plants via hardwood, semi-hardwood cuttings, and root division.
Can configure a Raspberry Pi to act as a localized weather station and automated irrigation controller.
Can build and maintain an aquaponics system balancing fish, biofilter bacteria, and plant growth.
Can read the landscape to identify historical water flows, even in highly urbanized environments.
Can frame and build a highly insulated, passive-solar tiny home on a movable trailer.
Can build a simple solar dehydrator to preserve local fruit and vegetable harvests.
Can effectively apply a tourniquet and perform wilderness first aid in a remote, off-grid setting.
Can observe and analyze the 'weeds' in an area to diagnose soil deficiencies without chemical testing.
Can build a fast-reacting compost pile (Berkeley Method) reaching 130-160°F within 4 days.
Can graft scion wood onto rootstock using whip-and-tongue or cleft grafting techniques.
Can install and configure a P2P mesh network communication device (e.g., Meshtastic) for off-grid comms.
Can operate heavy machinery (excavator/skid steer) with the finesse required for delicate permaculture earthworks.
Can perform a rapid bioblitz to categorize and document hyper-local biodiversity using citizen science tools.
Can operate and maintain open-source micro-manufacturing tools (e.g., 3D printers, CNC) for local repair economies.
Can set up and manage a multi-bay thermophilic composting system for community food waste.
Can write a Python script to scrape and analyze local municipal environmental open data.
Can operate a drone to capture NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) mapping of a farm.
Can design and implement a rotational grazing plan for a small herd to maximize carbon drawdown.
Can install a micro-hydro turbine in a local stream to generate baseload power.
Can operate a portable sawmill to process fallen local timber into dimensional lumber.
Can set up an open-source Nextcloud server to provide an alternative to big-tech data hosting.
Can design and implement a holistic grazing plan using electric fencing to mimic natural herd movements.
Can utilize GIS software (like QGIS) to map local watersheds and flood zones.

H3_Radical.text: Can cultivate Indigenous Microorganism (IMO) solutions for localized soil remediation and fertility enhancement.
Can forage and process 10+ local wild-edible plants safely for community consumption.
Can identify, harvest, and process 5 local medicinal herbs into tinctures or salves.
Can safely capture, process, and utilize humanure in a closed-loop sanitation system.
Can inoculate logs with shiitake or oyster mushroom spawn for localized food production.
Can process acorns or other local complex forage staples to remove tannins and create flour.
Can safely ferment vegetables (kraut, kimchi) to preserve the local harvest without refrigeration.
Can extract, save, and properly store seeds from open-pollinated heirloom tomato varieties.
Can identify 20 local bird species by sight and song to track ecosystem health changes.
Can weave a basic basket or container using local invasive vines (e.g., kudzu, english ivy).
Can maintain a sourdough starter and bake bread using locally milled heritage grains.
Can shear a sheep and process the raw fleece into spinnable roving.
Can use a scythe to clear tall grass and process it into mulch without fossil fuels.
Can safely catch, dispatch, and process a small local game animal or fish for consumption.
Can construct a 'Waru Waru' or similar raised bed system to manage frost and flood in extreme climates.
Can safely forage and prepare 3 local varieties of wild mushrooms, distinguishing them from toxic look-alikes.
Can weave a fishing net or hammock using paracord or natural twine.
Can brew actively aerated compost tea (AACT) using a microscope to verify fungal/bacterial ratios.
Can navigate by stars, sun, and landscape features without the use of GPS or digital technology.
Can safely extract and process local clay to throw simple pottery or create cob building materials.
Can process raw fleece, card it, spin it into yarn, and knit/weave a functional garment.
Can set up a LoRaWAN gateway to receive data from local agricultural soil moisture sensors.
Can operate a gas spectrometer to measure real-time soil respiration and carbon flux.
Can accurately read local topographical maps to design passive water-harvesting earthworks (swales, berms).

H1_Hospicing.text: Can use Nonviolent Communication (NVC) to de-escalate tension during direct environmental action.
Can effectively translate complex ecological science into culturally resonant storytelling for local policy-makers.
Can facilitate a grief circle to help a community process the loss of local ecosystems or species.
Can gracefully decommission a sunsetting organizational project while capturing and transferring its core learnings.
Can facilitate a privilege walk adapted specifically for ecological access and land ownership.
Can translate 'corporate sustainability jargon' into accessible, engaging language for a working-class neighborhood.
Can identify and gracefully interrupt microaggressions during a community assembly.
Can practice deep canvassing techniques to engage climate deniers in empathetic, shifting conversations.
Can adapt communication styles (e.g., DISC, Enneagram) to bridge divides between disparate community stakeholders.
Can maintain a neutral, multi-partial stance when facilitating highly polarized environmental disputes.
Can tactfully dismantle purity culture and gatekeeping within vegan/zero-waste activist spaces.
Can navigate the tension between 'professionalized' NGO culture and raw, grassroots activist culture.
Can design a community mural process that genuinely incorporates the visions of unhoused residents.
Can teach the principles of 'Nonviolent Direct Action' (NVDA) to a group of first-time protesters.
Can write a compelling press release that highlights the systemic roots of a local ecological disaster.
Can host a 'Speak-Out' or testimony circle for frontline communities experiencing environmental racism.
Can design a grassroots media campaign that uses humor and satire to delegitimize fossil fuel greenwashing.

H2_Bridging.text: Can mediate intergenerational conflicts regarding land-use transitions using restorative justice circle practices.
Can design and facilitate participatory onboarding processes that build immediate psychological safety in decentralized teams.
Can host an 'Art of Hosting' or 'World Cafe' session to align 50+ community members on a shared regenerative vision.
Can guide a group through an 'Ecovillage Playing Cards' session to gamify community design.
Can host a 'Death Cafe' to normalize conversations around mortality, collapse, and impermanence.
Can mediate a dispute between local farmers and conservationists by finding shared underlying values.
Can co-create a 'community agreement' charter that goes beyond rules to establish shared behavioral norms.
Can facilitate a systemic constellation exercise to map hidden dynamics within an activist group.
Can recognize and mitigate 'founder's syndrome' behaviors within the cultural fabric of an NGO.
Can foster an environment of 'radical candor' where critical feedback is given and received as an act of care.
Can hold space for the tension between 'urgent action' and 'slowing down to build trust' in activist circles.
Can organize a neighborhood 'Buy Nothing' or barter economy to build local trust networks.
Can facilitate a 'Fishbowl' conversation to bring marginalized voices to the center of a town hall.
Can identify when a group is stuck in 'analysis paralysis' and skillfully shift the energy toward prototyping.
Can use somatic theater or 'Theater of the Oppressed' techniques to explore power dynamics in a food co-op.
Can create a culture of 'working in the open' by transparently sharing organizational failures and learnings.
Can translate complex tokenomic/Web3 structures into culturally understandable concepts for a rural community.
Can design a mutual aid network that prioritizes dignity and solidarity over charity and pity.
Can cultivate an organizational culture that defaults to asynchronous communication to respect deep work and rest.
Can facilitate a 'Makerspace' workshop teaching basic soldering and electronic repair to youth.
Can weave an understanding of intersectional feminism into the core culture of a male-dominated tech-activist group.
Can design a playful, non-coercive incentive system to encourage neighbors to separate compostable waste.
Can create a culturally safe space for BIPOC farmers to share knowledge outside of white-dominated agricultural spaces.
Can curate a local 'Library of Things' catalog to make sharing tools and appliances culturally normative.
Can facilitate a 'Way of Council' circle to bypass intellectual debate and speak from the heart.
Can design a community skill-share network that bypasses monetary exchange completely.
Can host a 'PechaKucha' night focused exclusively on local regenerative failures and lessons learned.
Can use 'Deep Democracy' techniques to give voice to the 'terrorist' or 'saboteur' roles within a group unconscious.
Can facilitate a 'Bohmian Dialogue' to suspend assumptions and create shared meaning without seeking consensus.
Can use Open Space Technology to self-organize a 3-day bioregional unconference.
Can design a 'Truth and Reconciliation' process for local communities harmed by historical extractive industries.
Can decode the underlying metaphors used in local politics and reframe them toward regenerative outcomes.
Can curate an art exhibition that transforms eco-anxiety into actionable, localized hope.
Can design and facilitate a 'Scenario Planning' workshop to explore 4 distinct climate futures for a city.
Can mediate a community discussion on managed retreat from coastal areas facing inevitable sea-level rise.
Can use storytelling to bridge the gap between urban activists and rural agricultural workers.
Can facilitate a process of 'Collaborative Sensemaking' using tools like Pol.is to find consensus in large groups.
Can coordinate a decentralized network of 'Transition Hubs' without establishing a central hierarchy.

H3_Radical.text: Can teach complex systems thinking concepts to elementary school children using games and nature walks.
Can design and enact a seasonal rite-of-passage ceremony for youth entering ecological stewardship.
Can craft a compelling narrative 'mythos' for a startup that roots its purpose in deep ecological time.
Can conduct an oral history interview with local elders to document pre-industrial ecological baselines.
Can design a collective celebration that honors the mundane, hidden labor of community maintenance.
Can lead a community choir or drum circle to synchronize group brainwaves before a strategic planning session.
Can facilitate a 'Council of All Beings' exercise to build empathy for non-human life forms.
Can lead a 'soundwalk' to help citizens tune into the acoustic ecology of their neighborhood.
Can facilitate a collaborative 'Dreaming Session' to envision a post-carbon city neighborhood.
Can foster 'Warm Data' (Bateson) spaces where complex, inter-relational qualitative data can be shared.
Can synthesize diverse cultural narratives into a cohesive, motivating bioregional identity and mythology.
Can establish and nurture deep, reciprocal relationships with local Indigenous elders and knowledge keepers.
Can weave local folklore, art, and music into a public presentation on watershed health.
Can navigate the cultural nuances of requesting permission to access or restore Indigenous-owned land.
Can foster 'epistemic justice' by ensuring indigenous science is weighed equally with western academic science.
Can create a bioregional 'calendar' based on local phenology (blooms, migrations) rather than Gregorian months.
Can facilitate a conflict resolution process specifically designed for decentralized DAOs or remote networks.
Can foster a culture of 'elderhood' by systematically seeking out and valuing the wisdom of older generations.
Can translate 'Rights of Nature' into actionable indigenous cultural protocols for local land users.

H1_Hospicing.text: Can identify and apply for specific USDA or EU agricultural grants supporting transition to agroforestry.
Can audit an organization's bank accounts and transition funds to credit unions or fossil-free banks.
Can create a dynamic pricing model for a community-supported agriculture (CSA) program based on income tiers.
Can create a transition roadmap for a hospital to achieve zero-waste operations within 10 years.
Can draft an ecological 'Bill of Material' for a consumer product, tracing all elements back to the earth.
Can map the metabolic material flows (water, energy, waste) through a specific urban neighborhood to identify circular interventions.
Can conduct a life cycle assessment (LCA) to audit the true cost of legacy supply chains.
Can conduct a supply chain audit to identify Scope 3 emissions for a mid-sized regional business.
Can map the influence of corporate lobbying within a specific regional environmental regulatory body.
Can audit a city's public transit network to propose micro-mobility integrations to solve the 'last mile' problem.
Can evaluate carbon offset projects for 'additionality' and 'permanence' to avoid greenwashed solutions.
Can map the flow of venture capital in a region to expose the financial drivers of local gentrification.
Can calculate the EROI (Energy Return on Investment) of a proposed transition project to ensure viability.
Can map the 'Food Swamp' and 'Food Desert' metrics of a city to propose targeted urban agriculture interventions.
Can audit a municipality's pension fund to identify fossil fuel exposures and propose divestment strategies.
Can map the global supply chain of a specific critical mineral (e.g., cobalt, lithium) to identify systemic vulnerabilities.
Can structure a municipal 'Carbon Budget' aligned with 1.5C, breaking it down into departmental quotas.

H2_Bridging.text: Can draft legal bylaws and governance protocols for a multi-stakeholder platform cooperative.
Can structure a blended-finance funding model stacking grants, impact investments, and local crowd-equity.
Can implement Sociocracy or Holacracy decision-making frameworks within a rapidly scaling transition organization.
Can construct a diversified, non-extractive investment portfolio for a community trust.
Can map out the legal steps required to transition an LLC into a worker-owned cooperative.
Can structure a Timebank system to capture and exchange non-monetized labor within a neighborhood.
Can set up a local tool library, including liability waivers, inventory tracking, and membership structures.
Can set up the organizational infrastructure (Slack, Notion, Loom) for a globally distributed, asynchronous transition team.
Can design a local mesh network to provide decentralized internet access to a neighborhood.
Can write procurement policies for a university that mandate sourcing 30% of food from local regenerative farms.
Can create an adaptive management plan for a conservation area that adjusts to real-time climate feedback.
Can structure a regional seed library with protocols for tracking provenance, germination rates, and fair distribution.
Can establish a 'Transition Town' steering committee utilizing sociocratic circle structures.
Can structure an 'Evergreen Fund' model to finance regenerative startups without requiring a liquidity exit.
Can map the causal loops, feedback delays, and leverage points in a local bioregional food and waste system.
Can identify leverage points in municipal zoning codes to advocate for regenerative housing developments.
Can map the regional flow of organic waste and match it as an input for local agricultural projects.
Can draft municipal zoning amendment proposals that legalize composting toilets and greywater systems.
Can map out an emergency localized supply chain for essential medicines and food in case of grid failure.
Can structure a 'Payment for Ecosystem Services' (PES) program between a city and upstream farmers.
Can analyze municipal budgets to identify funds that can be reallocated through Participatory Budgeting.
Can conduct a 'leverage point analysis' (per Donella Meadows) on a local affordable housing crisis.
Can design an insurance pool mechanism for local farmers to mutualize risk against extreme weather events.
Can map the historical enclosures of the commons in a specific region to inform strategies for 'commoning' new resources.
Can structure a crowdfunded community buyout of a local failing, fossil-dependent utility.
Can model the systemic economic shock of a localized grid failure and design mitigation strategies.
Can draft an open-source hardware license that prevents corporate patenting of community-developed eco-tech.
Can architect a regional transit system that prioritizes non-motorized and public transport over private EVs.
Can structure a 'Dark Matter' analysis to find the invisible regulatory/bureaucratic blockers to local climate action.
Can draft an open-source agreement for a hardware cooperative to share CNC routing files globally.
Can structure a 'Platform Cooperative' for gig workers (e.g., delivery drivers) to own their own dispatch app.
Can analyze the systemic impacts of Universal Basic Income (UBI) on local ecological transition rates.
Can design a local 'Cap and Share' program for fossil fuel rationing at the municipal level.
Can structure an open-source hardware cooperative specifically for producing appropriate agricultural tech.
Can design a bioregional transition strategy that integrates H1 hospicing, H2 bridge-building, and H3 radical innovation.

H3_Radical.text: Can create a comprehensive land plan for an area (e.g., an ecovillage) integrating water, energy, shelter, and food systems.
Can draft an 'open-value network' accounting system to track and reward contributions in a decentralized project.
Can structure a legal 'Stewardship Ownership' model (e.g., golden share) to permanently protect a company's regenerative mission.
Can draft an overarching constitution for an ecovillage outlining dispute resolution, membership, and ecological limits.
Can set up a local 'Energy Community' legal framework allowing neighbors to trade solar power peer-to-peer.
Can design a tokenomic model or complementary currency that explicitly incentivizes carbon sequestration and biodiversity net gain.
Can architect an open-source data commons for sharing bioregional ecological health metrics.
Can draft an operating agreement for a Community Land Trust (CLT) that removes land from the speculative market.
Can design a 'smart contract' on a blockchain to automate payouts for verified regenerative agricultural practices.
Can implement the 'Doughnut Economics' city portrait methodology for a specific municipality.
Can design a governance structure using 'Liquid Democracy' principles for a decentralized network.
Can calculate the localized carrying capacity and bio-capacity of a specific watershed.
Can architect a 'Data Trust' to steward local ecological data on behalf of a community, preventing corporate extraction.
Can legally structure an 'inter-species trust' that gives legal personhood and financial assets to a river or forest.
Can design an algorithmic reputation system for a DAO that rewards pro-social, regenerative behavior over capital investment.
Can structure a bioregional assembly of citizens drawn by sortition (lottery) to deliberate on climate action.
Can design a 'circular fashion' localized supply chain integrating local fiber production, milling, and garment repair.
Can design an algorithmic framework to track and internalize externalized social costs in a corporate balance sheet.
Can design a 'Rights of Nature' legal framework for a local municipality to protect a specific ecosystem.
Can design a tokenomic airdrop that specifically rewards early contributors to an open-source ecology project.
Can design a 'Transition Currency' that decays in value over time (demurrage) to stimulate local circulation.
Can design a 'Bioregional State' governance model based on watersheds rather than arbitrary colonial borders.
Can structure a 'Dark Pool' carbon credit mechanism that prevents speculative trading of ecological assets.
Can draft a 'Stewardship Protocol' for an AI system to ensure its objectives align with bioregional health.
Can design an algorithmic 'Smart Contract' that automatically funds local climate adaptation based on weather triggers.
